semanticCore: Burning brightly, glowing intensely, radiating heat/light, flaming, or solar brightness

keyDerivations.meaning: Burning brightly, glowing intensely, radiating strong heat/light, or blazing

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَجَعَلْنَا سِرَاجًا وَهَّاجًا (78:13): And We made a burning, bright lamp (referring to the sun).
summaryEn: The root represents bright, heat-emitting illumination (Wahj), describing the sun (Siraj Wahhaj) as a primary source of cosmic light and life-giving heat for the earth.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر شدة التوقد والإضاءة والحرارة والالتهاب المشع نورا وحرارة (وهج)، واصفاً الشمس بالسراج المتوهج الناشر للدفيء والضياء في الكون.
rawText: اوهج 4 He kindled a fire; made it to burn; syn. أَوْقَدَ: (S, K;) [or made it to burn fiercely, or to glow:] in the M, 🡻وهّج. (L.) توهّج 5 : see 1. === ‡ It (the odour of perfume) was hot, or strong; syn. توقّد. (S, K.) === ‡ It (a jewel) shone; glistened; glowed. (S, K.) === ‡ It (a day) was violently hot. (A.) === ‡ It (heat) was violent. (A.) مُتَوَهِّجَةٌ , as an epithet applied to a woman, Hot in the pudendum; expl. by حَارَّةُ المَتَاع. (L.) نَجْمٌ وَهَّاجٌ [A fiercely burning, or glowing, star]. (TA.) سِرَاجٌ وَهَّاجٌ [A fiercely burning, or glowing, lamp: Kur. lxxviii. 13:] i. e., the sun. (TA.) وَهَجٌ and 🡻وَهِيجٌ The burning, or heat, of fire. (S, K.) [See 1.] === Also, The diffusion (in a neut. sense) of the odour of perfume; and the hot, or strong, odour, thereof. (L.) === Also, the latter, The shining, glistening, or glowing, of a jewel. (L.) وَهَجَانٌ and وَمَجَانَةٌ: see وَهِجٌ. وَهَجَتِ النَّارُ 1 , aor. تَهِجُ, inf. n. وَهْجٌ and وَهَجَانٌ; (S, L, K;) and وَهَجَتَ, [app. a mistake for وَهِجَت,] aor. تَوْهَجُ, [inf. n. وَهَجٌ;] (L;) The fire burned; syn. اِتَّقَدَت; (S, K, &c.;) and so 🡻تَوهّجت; (L;) syn. توقّدت; (S, L, K;) [or this last has an intensive sense, and signifies, as does the first accord. to the A, it burned fiercely; glowed]. === Also, all these verbs, with the same inf. ns., It (the sun) burned, or was hot: and it (a fire) burned from a distance. (L.) === Also, all the above verbs, with the same inf. ns., ‡ It (a day, and a night,) was violently hot. (L.) وَهِيجٌ : see وَهَجٌ. وَهَّجَ 2 see 4. يَوْمٌ وَهِجٌ , and 🡻وَهَجَانٌ, and لَيْلَةٌ وَهِجَةٌ, and 🡻وَهَجَانَةٌ, ‡ A violently hot day, and night. (L.)
